activities.markdown:
# 5 Positions of the Pentatonic

The minor pentatonic has 5 positions that cover the whole neck. Position 1 (the box) is your foundation.

**Position 2** starts 3 frets up from Position 1:
```
e |--8--10--|
B |--8--10--|
G |--7--10--|
D |--7--10--|
A |--7-10--|
E |--8--10--|
```

Connect positions by sliding from one to the next — your solos will start spanning the whole fretboard.

activities.question: How many positions does the minor pentatonic scale have across the fretboard?
activities.explanation: The minor pentatonic has 5 positions that tile across the entire neck. Most players start with Position 1 (the box) and gradually learn the others.
